question_text: A student observes two different waves: Wave X is a sound wave travelling through air, and Wave Y is a light wave travelling through a vacuum. Which of the following statements correctly compares these two waves?

options.label: A
options.text: Wave X is transverse and requires a medium; Wave Y is longitudinal and does not require a medium

options.label: B
options.text: Wave X is longitudinal and requires a medium; Wave Y is transverse and does not require a medium

options.label: C
options.text: Both waves are transverse and both require a medium to travel

options.label: D
options.text: Both waves are longitudinal and neither requires a medium to travel

correct_answer: B) Wave X is longitudinal and requires a medium; Wave Y is transverse and does not require a medium
explanation: Sound waves are longitudinal waves that vibrate parallel to the direction of travel and need a medium like air to propagate. Light waves are transverse waves that vibrate perpendicular to their direction of travel and can travel through a vacuum without any medium. Understanding these fundamental differences is crucial for distinguishing between different wave types.
